_comment: Example pgcheck config. JSON does not support real comments; keys starting with _comment are ignored by pgcheck.

connection._comment: Connection settings. These are equivalent to PGHOST, PGPORT, PGUSER, PGPASSWORD, PGDATABASE and PGSSLMODE.

psql._comment: These options mirror psql command-line flags. They only affect the psql backend; the native backend applies compatible display behavior where possible.
psql._quiet: quiet=true passes -q to psql, reducing non-data messages.
psql._tuples_only: tuples_only=true passes -t to psql, showing rows without headers/footers.
psql._no_align: no_align=true passes -A to psql, using unaligned output.
psql._no_psqlrc: no_psqlrc=true passes -X to psql, preventing ~/.psqlrc from changing output.
psql._extra_args: extra_args are appended directly to psql, for advanced options such as --csv or --set name=value.

output._expanded: expanded controls pgcheck display: auto keeps command defaults, table forces table view, expanded forces psql -x style output.
